title: QUIC CRT client-side abbreviated resumption completion architecture
purpose: Describe how the managed client/runtime path completes an accepted PSK-capable ClientHello attempt through the abbreviated TLS 1.3 resumption flight after ServerHello while rejected attempts remain on the full-handshake fallback path.
risks: If the accepted PSK branch still expects Certificate or CertificateVerify, the abbreviated completion path will fail closed before Active promotion.
If the client bridge-state completion gates remain certificate-bound, the runtime will never observe the accepted branch as a completed handshake.
If rejected attempts stop using the existing fallback path, the slice will silently corrupt the non-resumption client flow.
If the abbreviated path widens public promises, the slice will overclaim resumed-handshake support or feature completeness.
design_summary: The accepted PSK branch is treated as a separate client-side abbreviated transcript shape. The transcript progress owner records ServerHello PSK acceptance, switches the accepted branch to expect EncryptedExtensions followed by Finished, and leaves rejected attempts on the existing full-handshake transcript path. The key schedule continues to derive handshake and application traffic secrets from the accepted abbreviated transcript, and the transport bridge state treats accepted PSK disposition as sufficient for peer transcript completion and transport-parameter commit without requiring certificate verification. Early data remains explicitly closed and the public surface does not widen.
alternatives_considered: Keep the accepted branch as a terminal branch-point only. Rejected because it would leave the client unable to complete the abbreviated handshake truthfully.
Require certificate verification even on accepted PSK resumption. Rejected because it would not match the abbreviated TLS 1.3 resumption structure the accepted branch earns.
Introduce a new public resumption API surface. Rejected because this slice is internal-runtime only and must not widen the consumer promise.
edge_cases_and_constraints: The accepted branch must stay client-role only.
The abbreviated transcript must stay closed to early data.
Rejected attempts must keep the current full-handshake fallback behavior unchanged.
The accepted path must not claim 0-RTT, anti-replay, key update, or public resumption support beyond completion of the abbreviated handshake.
The bridge-state gates must not reintroduce certificate verification requirements onto the accepted abbreviated path.
data_and_state_considerations: The client runtime already carries dormant detached resumption material and a resumption attempt disposition bit. The new accepted branch uses that disposition to choose the abbreviated transcript shape and to allow transcript-completion and peer-transport-parameter commit without the certificate verification state that full handshakes require. The runtime still captures the resumption master secret after the peer Finished message so the detached carrier and later dormant state remain usable for future attempts.

supplemental_sections.heading: Scope
supplemental_sections.content: This slice is client-role only, managed-runtime only, and limited to accepted PSK completion after ServerHello. It does not add 0-RTT, anti-replay, key update, or public resumption claims.
